name: Oxidative-stress sensor (OxyR + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: An ingestible sensor for hydrogen peroxide / oxidative stress, a marker of gut inflammation. The native OxyR peroxide sensor gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the probiotic E. coli Nissle 1917.

input.detects: Luminal hydrogen peroxide and reactive oxygen produced during gut inflammation

sensing.sensorMechanism: OxyR is oxidized by H2O2 and, in its oxidized form, activates antioxidant promoters such as PkatG / PahpC.
sensing.signalFlow: H2O2 -> oxidized OxyR activates PahpC -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence reports oxidative stress.

parts.name: OxyR regulator
parts.notes: H2O2-responsive transcriptional activator; endogenous to E. coli Nissle.

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak reporter promoter

parts.name: sfGFP reporter
parts.notes: Recoverable from stool for non-invasive readout.

output.result: Fluorescent-cell fraction rises with luminal oxidative stress.

performance.notes: OxyR peroxide sensing and CRISPRa are validated separately; integrated device is a design template. Complements the thiosulfate/tetrathionate inflammation sensors with an oxidative-stress marker.

safety.biocontainment: Probiotic E. coli Nissle host; add thyA/dapA auxotrophy for gut-restricted containment.
safety.notes: Probiotic chassis with a human-safety record; research / supervised clinical use only.

buildSteps.title: Design CRISPRa sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the activation window of a weak reporter promoter; check Nissle off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: PahpC -> sgRNA (native OxyR). TU2: dCas9-omega. TU3: weak promoter -> sfGFP. Use a stable low-copy vector.

buildSteps.title: Validate with H2O2
buildSteps.description: Confirm peroxide-dependent activation across a standard curve before any animal work.

buildSteps.title: Recover and measure
buildSteps.description: Recover cells from stool; quantify fluorescence.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native OxyR peroxide sensor with a dCas9 CRISPRa circuit in E. coli Nissle
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
